feed.title: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository
feed.subtitle: Security Advisory for Java artifacts hosted at the Maven central repository on GitHub

entry.title: [org.cyclonedx:cyclonedx-core-java] CycloneDX Core (Java): BOM validation is vulnerable to XML External Entity injection
entry.content:
Impact

The XML Validator used by cyclonedx-core-java was not configured securely, making the library vulnerable to XML External Entity (XXE) injection.

The fix for GHSA-683x-4444-jxh8 / CVE-2024-38374 has been incomplete in that it only fixed parsing of XML BOMs, but not validation.

Patches

The vulnerability has been fixed in cyclonedx-core-java version 11.0.1.

Workarounds

If feasible, applications can reject XML documents before handing them to cyclonedx-core-java for validation. This may be an option if incoming CycloneDX BOMs are known to be in JSON format.

References

  • The issue was introduced via https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-core-java/commit/162aa594f347b3f612fe0a45071693c3cd398ce9
  • The issue was fixed via https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-core-java/pull/737
  • https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/XML_External_Entity_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html#schemafactory

References

  • https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-core-java/security/advisories/GHSA-6fhj-vr9j-g45r
  • https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-core-java/pull/737
  • https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-core-java/commit/162aa594f347b3f612fe0a45071693c3cd398ce9
  • https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-core-java/commit/af0ec75c93c03f93733a070c5132554490af5314
  • https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/XML_External_Entity_Prevention_Cheat_Sheet.html#schemafactory
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6fhj-vr9j-g45r

entry.title: [org.apache.solr:solr-core] Incorrect Authorization in Apache Solr
entry.content:
In Apache Solr, the cluster can be partitioned into multiple collections and only a subset of nodes actually host any given collection. However, if a node receives a request for a collection it does not host, it proxies the request to a relevant node and serves the request. Solr bypasses all authorization settings for such requests. This affects all Solr versions prior to 6.6.6 and 7.7 that use the default authorization mechanism of Solr (RuleBasedAuthorizationPlugin).

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2018-11802
  • https://www.openwall.com/lists/oss-security/2019/04/24/1
  • https://github.com/apache/lucene-solr/commit/add003f217806afb4e1604f697cdb0a5a7115895
  • https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/SOLR-12514
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-j346-h5wc-rw2m

entry.title: [org.apache.solr:solr-core] Incorrect Authorization in Apache Solr
entry.content:
In Apache Solr, the cluster can be partitioned into multiple collections and only a subset of nodes actually host any given collection. However, if a node receives a request for a collection it does not host, it proxies the request to a relevant node and serves the request. Solr bypasses all authorization settings for such requests. This affects all Solr versions prior to 6.6.6 and 7.7 that use the default authorization mechanism of Solr (RuleBasedAuthorizationPlugin).

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2018-11802
  • https://www.openwall.com/lists/oss-security/2019/04/24/1
  • https://github.com/apache/lucene-solr/commit/add003f217806afb4e1604f697cdb0a5a7115895

entry.content:
In Apache Solr, the cluster can be partitioned into multiple collections and only a subset of nodes actually host any given collection. However, if a node receives a request for a collection it does not host, it proxies the request to a relevant node and serves the request. Solr bypasses all authorization settings for such requests. This affects all Solr versions prior to 6.6.6 and 7.7 that use the default authorization mechanism of Solr (RuleBasedAuthorizationPlugin).

entry.title: [org.apache.solr:solr-parent] Incorrect Authorization in Apache Solr
entry.content:
In Apache Solr, the cluster can be partitioned into multiple collections and only a subset of nodes actually host any given collection. However, if a node receives a request for a collection it does not host, it proxies the request to a relevant node and serves the request. Solr bypasses all authorization settings for such requests. This affects all Solr versions prior to 6.6.6 and 7.7 that use the default authorization mechanism of Solr (RuleBasedAuthorizationPlugin).

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2018-11802
  • https://www.openwall.com/lists/oss-security/2019/04/24/1
  • https://github.com/apache/lucene-solr/commit/add003f217806afb4e1604f697cdb0a5a7115895

  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-j346-h5wc-rw2m

entry.title: [co.fs2:fs2-io_3] FS2 half-shutdown of socket during TLS handshake may result in spin loop on opposite side
entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

Workarounds

No workarounds.

For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

Open an issue. Contact the Typelevel Security Team.

References

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/security/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/issues/3590
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/46e2dc3abf994dcf3d0b804b2ddb3c10c04d4976
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/5c6c4c6c1ef330f7e6b53661ecc63d5f5ba8885c
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.12.2
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.13.0-M7
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-58369
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/pull/3624
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/edf0c4f2e660360d1c1a8c5377ce32294de89238
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

entry.title: [co.fs2:fs2-io_2.13] FS2 half-shutdown of socket during TLS handshake may result in spin loop on opposite side
entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

Workarounds

No workarounds.

For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

Open an issue. Contact the Typelevel Security Team.

References

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.12.2
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.13.0-M7
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-58369
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/pull/3624

  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

entry.title: [co.fs2:fs2-io_2.12] FS2 half-shutdown of socket during TLS handshake may result in spin loop on opposite side
entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

No workarounds.

For more information

entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

entry.title: [co.fs2:fs2-io_2.12.0-RC2] FS2 half-shutdown of socket during TLS handshake may result in spin loop on opposite side
entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

Workarounds

No workarounds.

For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

Open an issue. Contact the Typelevel Security Team.

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/5c6c4c6c1ef330f7e6b53661ecc63d5f5ba8885c
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.12.2
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.13.0-M7
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-58369
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/pull/3624
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/edf0c4f2e660360d1c1a8c5377ce32294de89238
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

entry.title: [co.fs2:fs2-io_3] FS2 half-shutdown of socket during TLS handshake may result in spin loop on opposite side
entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

Workarounds

No workarounds.

For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

Open an issue. Contact the Typelevel Security Team.

References

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/security/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/issues/3590
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/46e2dc3abf994dcf3d0b804b2ddb3c10c04d4976
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/5c6c4c6c1ef330f7e6b53661ecc63d5f5ba8885c
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.12.2
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.13.0-M7
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-58369
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/pull/3624
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/commit/edf0c4f2e660360d1c1a8c5377ce32294de89238
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

entry.title: [co.fs2:fs2-io_2.13] FS2 half-shutdown of socket during TLS handshake may result in spin loop on opposite side
entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

Workarounds

No workarounds.

For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

Open an issue. Contact the Typelevel Security Team.

References

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/security/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.13.0-M7
  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-58369
  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/pull/3624

  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

entry.title: [co.fs2:fs2-io_2.12] FS2 half-shutdown of socket during TLS handshake may result in spin loop on opposite side
entry.content:
Impact

When establishing a TLS session using fs2-io on the JVM using the fs2.io.net.tls package, if one side of the connection shuts down write while the peer side is awaiting more data to progress the TLS handshake, the peer side will spin loop on the socket read, fully utilizing a CPU. This CPU is consumed until the overall connection is closed.

This could be used as a denial of service attack on an fs2-io powered server -- for example, by opening many connections and putting them in a half-shutdown state.

Note: this issue impacts ember backed http4s servers with HTTPS as a result of ember using fs2's TLS support.

Patches

Fixed in fs2 3.12.2 and 3.13.0-M7.

Workarounds

No workarounds.

For more information

If you have any questions or comments about this advisory:

Open an issue. Contact the Typelevel Security Team.

References

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/security/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

  • https://github.com/typelevel/fs2/releases/tag/v3.13.0-M7

  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-rrw2-px9j-qffj

entry.title: [org.apereo.cas:cas-management-webapp-support] Apereo CAS has inefficient regular expression complexity
entry.content:
A vulnerability was found in Apereo CAS 5.2.6. It has been classified as problematic. This affects the function ResponseEntity of the file cas-5.2.6\webapp-mgmt\cas-management-webapp-support\src\main\java\org\apereo\cas\mgmt\services\web\ManageRegisteredServicesMultiActionController.java. The manipulation of the argument Query leads to inefficient regular expression complexity. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

References

  • https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-3985
  • https://vuldb.com/?ctiid.306321
  • https://vuldb.com/?id.306321
  • https://vuldb.com/?submit.557110
  • https://wx.mail.qq.com/s?k=lzDuxVkSRXUZ0bwZEG
  • https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8rx4-fxq5-vj4v
